question_text: A new hydroelectric dam costs £3 billion to build and lasts 50 years. Annual operating costs are £50 million. A coal power station costs £1.5 billion, lasts 40 years, and costs £300 million per year in fuel and maintenance. Calculate the average annual cost for each and explain which is cheaper over their lifetime.
correct_answer: Hydroelectric: £110 million/year (3000 + 2500 ÷ 50). Coal: £337.5 million/year (1500 + 12000 ÷ 40). Hydroelectric is cheaper overall despite higher upfront cost, and produces no emissions.
explanation: Divide total cost (upfront plus operating costs over lifetime) by years: Hydro = (3000 + 50×50) ÷ 50 = 3250 ÷ 50 = £65 million/year. Coal = (1500 + 300×40) ÷ 40 = 13500 ÷ 40 = £337.5 million/year. Hydroelectric is much cheaper per year and has environmental benefits.
